# THE ORACLE — K-Fortune & Feast

> THE ORACLE is a free, multilingual Korean fortune-telling web application based on the ancient Saju (사주, Four Pillars of Destiny) system. It provides personalized destiny readings, romantic compatibility checks, and group fortune readings for a global K-culture audience.

## Site Identity
- URL: https://linefortune.com/
- Brand: THE ORACLE
- Domain: linefortune.com
- Languages: Korean (한국어), English, Indonesian (Bahasa Indonesia), Spanish (Español)
- Primary Topic: Korean fortune telling (Saju), K-culture, shamanic wisdom, divination
- Audience: K-pop fans, K-culture enthusiasts, people curious about Asian spiritual traditions
- Cost: Free, no account required

## Main Pages

- [Home — Fortune Reading](https://linefortune.com/): Free Saju destiny reading, compatibility check, group fortune reading tool
- [Blog — Tales from Three Worlds](https://linefortune.com/blog.html): Original fairy-tale stories comparing Korean, Brazilian, and Indonesian fortune traditions
- [About](https://linefortune.com/about.html): The story and mission of THE ORACLE team
- [Contact](https://linefortune.com/contact.html): Contact the editorial team

## Core Features

1. **Fortune Reading (운세)** — Enter birth date, weight, and MBTI type to receive a personalized Korean destiny reading based on Saju
2. **Compatibility Check (궁합)** — Compare two people's birth information for romantic or friendship compatibility
3. **Group Reading (그룹)** — Get collective fortune readings for K-pop groups or friend groups

## Blog Content Topics

- Korean shamanism (무속 신앙): Mudang rituals, Samsin Halmoni, Susin, haemong (dream interpretation)
- Brazilian spiritual traditions: Candomblé Orixás (Nanã, Oxum, Xangô, Iemanjá, Oxalá, Egungun), Umbanda, terreiro
- Indonesian mysticism: Javanese Kejawen (Primbon, Wuku cycle, peksi, tafsir mimpi), Balinese Hindu ceremonies (Ngaben, Nyepi, Purnama), Dewi Sri
- Comparative themes: fate, dreams, fire purification, ancestor veneration, moon cycles, bird omens, color symbolism, numerology

## Published Blog Stories (as of 2026-04-21)

Stories 01–05 (2026-04-06):
- Three Grandmothers at the Crossroads of Fate
- The River That Remembers Your Name
- The Color That Chooses You
- The Lamp in the Fortune Teller's Window
- The Numbers That Found You First

Stories 06–10 (2026-04-21):
- The Dream That Arrived Before Morning
- The Fire That Knows Your Name
- The Ancestor Who Left a Chair Empty
- The Moon's Debt to the Tide
- The Bird at the Window at Dawn

## Editorial Standards

All content is original, human-written cultural storytelling. Stories are research-informed narratives drawing from documented traditions in Korean shamanism, Brazilian Candomblé/Umbanda, and Indonesian Kejawen/Balinese Hinduism. Content is intended as entertainment and cultural education — not professional spiritual, medical, or psychological advice.
